data.title: Bulls retreat from Bath & Body Works after a rough quarter
data.description:
Bath & Body Works (BBWI) saw a wave of downgrades on Friday after the retailer lowered its full-year profit guidance amid what it said has been a continuation of macro consumer pressures. During the earnings call, BBWI management noted that merchandise margin was negatively impacted by approximately $35 million, or roughly 200 basis points, from tariffs. The company also increased its promotional activity to clear seasonal product.

Firms lowering the ratings boom included Morgan Stanley (Equal Weight from Overweight), Telsey Advisory (Market Perform from Outperform), Goldman Sachs (Neutral from Buy), and Robert Baird (Neutral from Outperform).

Morgan Stanley analyst Alex Straton warned that the path for Bath & Body Works (BBWI) to consistently see solid sales growth and margin expansion appears to be increasingly unreachable. "Potentially structural topline headwinds & elevated investment mean BBWI could face ongoing topline declines & margin erosion from here," warned Straton.

Shares of Bath & Body Works (BBWI) trickled 0.8% lower after a 24.8% slide on Thursday that sent the stock to a new 52-week low of $15.23.

data.title: Heliostar Metals GAAP EPS of $0.01, revenue of $26.76M
data.description:
• Heliostar Metals press release (HSTXF): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.01.
  • Revenue of $26.76M.
  • The company said it is on track to achieve its annual production guidance of 31,000 to 41,000 GEOs, annual cash cost of $1,800-1,900 per GEO sold and AISC of $1,950-2,100 per GEO sold for 2025.

data.title: Japan approves restart of world's biggest nuclear power plant
data.description:
The world's biggest nuclear power plant is poised to restart after a Japanese regional governor cleared plans Friday to proceed with two reactors at Tokyo Electric's (OTCPK:TKECF) (OTCPK:TKECY) Kashiwazaki Kariwa plant.

The decision is the strongest signal yet that Japan is re-embracing nuclear power after the 2011 Fukushima Daiichi power plant meltdown, the world’s worst nuclear disaster since Chernobyl; after the disaster, Japan shut all 54 of its nuclear reactors in operation, leaving it heavily reliant on fossil fuel imports.

Japan spent ¥10.7 trillion ($68 billion) last year on imported liquefied natural gas and coal, 10% of its total import costs.

Once approved by the Niigata prefectural assembly, the decision would cover reactors No.6 and No.7, the two biggest at the Kashiwazaki Kariwa plant. 

As electricity demand is set to grow due to data center and semiconductor business expansion, "it would be difficult to stop something that passed the country's regulatory standards without any rational reason," Prefecture Governor Hideyo Hanazumi said.

ETFs: (NLR), (URA), (URNM)

data.title: Magna International and GAC to assemble AION V in Europe
data.description:
• Canada’s Magna International (MGA) and China’s GAC will together assemble GAC’s electric SUV, AION V, at Magna’s (MGA) assembly plant in Graz, Austria, accelerating GAC’s expansion in Europe and Magna’s (MGA) manufacturing capabilities.
  • Through partnerships such as its deal with Magna (MGA), Guangzhou-based GAC aims to broaden its European footprint beyond Finland, Portugal, and Poland — and sidestep EU tariffs on Chinese-built vehicles by assembling cars within Europe.
  • Magna (MGA) currently assembles the Xpeng (XPEV) G6 and G9 and Mercedes (OTCPK:MBGAF) (OTCPK:MBGYY) G-Class at its Graz facility, as well as ICE vehicles, the BMW (OTC:BMWKY) Z4 and Toyota (TM) Supra.  
  • Magna International (MGA) shares are catching a modest bid on the news and are set to snap a 6 day losing streak at Friday’s open.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: D-Wave Quantum says it completed redemption of all public warrants for common stock
data.description:
• D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) said on Friday that it has completed the redemption of all public warrants to purchase common stock.
  • Shares rose 1.5% in premarket trading.
  • Nearly 4.75M warrants were exercised after D-Wave's prior announcement, which represent approximately 6.9M shares of common stock at an exercise price of $11.50 per share. That resulted in roughly $54.6M in cash to D-Wave Quantum. 
  • After the redemption, 270,820 warrants were unexercised as of 5 p.m. EST yesterday, and D-Wave redeemed them for $0.01 per warrant. The warrants have now ceased trading and will be delisted from the New York Stock Exchange.

data.title: Novo, Lilly said to sell obesity drugs directly to employers, bypassing PBMs
data.description:
Novo Nordisk (NVO) and Eli Lilly (LLY) are set to introduce a new approach to expand access to weight loss drugs, selling the injectables directly to employers, thereby avoiding pharma middlemen known as pharmacy benefit managers, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.

Starting from Jan. 1, the pharma giants will offer their obesity drugs Wegovy and Zepbound through Waltz Health, a digital health company focused on helping employers buy affordable medications.

Under the offering, employers will procure the injectables by paying upfront, fixed prices without involving rebates and fees, which are usually paid to PBMs as part of traditional drug purchases.

Multiple companies have already signed up to join the model, Waltz CEO Mark Thierer said, adding, “This will be the absolute cheapest way” for those on employer benefit plans to get access to the weight loss drugs.

Employers typically rely on PBMs to negotiate drug prices for their sponsored health plans, which in the U.S. cater to about 165M workers. Leading PBMs include Cigna’s (CI) Express Scripts, CVS Health’s (CVS) Caremark, UnitedHealth’s (UNH) Optum Rx, and Elevance Health’s (ELV) CarelonRx.

According to Thierer, four companies have agreed to launch the model, which targets employers who currently do not cover the weight loss drugs. A recent survey from the healthcare policy organization KFF indicated that only about 43% of companies with over 5,000 employees offer coverage for weight loss drugs.

“This is meant to be an open-source marketplace where drugs compete on their clinical merits" and price, Thierer said, adding that drugmakers want “a level playing field.”

The model marks LLY and NVO’s latest effort aimed at expanding GLP-1 access since the launch of their direct-to-consumer sites, Novo Pharmacy and Lilly Direct, which offer the two blockbuster drugs and other medicines to cash-paying patients at discounted prices.

data.title: Coinbase to buy Vector, expanding support for solana network
data.description:
Crypto exchange Coinbase Global (COIN) agreed to acquire Vector, an onchain trading platform built on solana (SOL-USD), the company said on Friday.

Integrating Vector's technology into Coinbase's (COIN) consumer trading experience will give users broader access to onchain markets, it added. The deal is expected to close by year-end; financial terms weren't disclosed.

"This year, we laid out our vision to build the everything exchange: a one-stop-shop for trading everything onchain, offering faster, cheaper, more global, and 24/7 accessible markets," Coinbase said in a statement. "To do that, we’re doubling down on the ecosystems where users are building, trading, and experimenting the most – solana being a big part of that story."

The Vector technology will plug directly into Coinbase's (COIN) DEX trading integration to eventually improve speed, liquidity, and access to a broader set of assets across the solana ecosystem.

Coinbase (COIN) stock rose 0.6% in premarket trading.

data.title: AST SpaceMobile's next-gen BlueBird 6 satellite to launch on December 15
data.description:
AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) said its next-generation BlueBird 6 satellite is set to launch on December 15 from the Satish Dhawan Space Center in India.

When launched, BlueBird 6 will feature the largest commercial phased-array antenna in low Earth orbit, about 2,400 sq. ft., making it 3.5× bigger and 10× higher in data capacity than earlier models.

"Our next-generation satellites will soon enable ubiquitous cellular broadband coverage direct to everyday smartphones from space," said Abel Avellan, Founder, Chairman and CEO of AST SpaceMobile. "As an American company, we are proud to demonstrate U.S. leadership in space innovation while pioneering the next era of global connectivity."

The company is speeding up production, with 40 satellites equivalent of microns on track to be completed by early 2026.

It plans five orbital launches by the end of first quarter of 2026 and aims to have 45–60 satellites in orbit by the end of 2026 to enable continuous coverage in the U.S. and select markets.

ASTS shares jumped 6.6% premarket to $53.90.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Elastic slumps despite Q2 beat, raised outlook; analysts show mixed reactions
data.description:
Shares of Elastic (ESTC) fell about 12% premarket on Friday despite second-quarter results beating estimates and the company raising its full-year revenue outlook.

Wedbush maintained its Outperform rating on the Dutch company's stock but lowered the price target to $90 from $110.

"Last night, ESTC reported its FY2Q26 results which featured beats on the top and bottom lines while raising FY26 guidance/FY3Q26 come in ahead of the Street expectations but will be overshadowed by its recently metric, sales-led subscription revenue growth came in below the target established on Analyst Day in October," said analysts led by Daniel Ives.

The analysts noted that total revenues came in at $423.5M above both the Street’s estimate of $418.2M as the company continues to capitalize on broad-based demand for its solutions through continued execution with its Search AI platform. Customers spending over $100K in Annual Contract Value, or ACV, are now over 1,600 (up 13% year-over-year) as consumption trends across both AI-native and traditional customers remain healthy, the analysts added.

The Amsterdam-based company's Cloud revenue came in at $205.7M (up 22% year-over-year) representing almost 50% of total revenue (prior of 47%), just above the Street’s estimate of $203.2M as GenAI application development remains a key focus, with over 2,450 customers using Elastic Cloud for GenAI use cases (prior period at 2,200) with nearly 370 customers paying over $100K for GenAI (prior period of 330) as the company continues to see expansion of GenAI uses and further penetrating its existing customer base, the analysts noted.

Elastic raised its fiscal 2026 total revenue range to be between $1.715B and $1.721B (prior guidance of $1.679B to $1.689B) representing 15% year-over-year growth in constant currency ((cc)) along with sales-led subscription revenue of $1.417B to $1.423B (up 17% year-over-year in CC) as the company looks to capitalize on growing demand for its platform approach with more customers looking to consolidate spend from legacy vendors to next-gen solutions, the analysts added.

"While this quarter was a speed bump to the ESTC growth story and we believe the company is in the early innings of its longterm growth story as it continues [to] invest strategically to capture more share of the AI market," said Ives and his team.

Meanwhile, KeyBanc Capital Markets kept its Sector Weight rating on Elastic's stock.

"Solid F2Q beat & raise, though cloud revenue a touch softer than expected and investors needing little reason to sell observability/SIEM names post the Chronosphere acq. [acquisition]," said analysts led by Eric Heath.

Update: The story was updated with KeyBanc analysts' commentary.

data.title: VCI Global CEO purchases shares on the open market
data.description:
• VCI Global (VCIG) on Friday said that its Group Executive Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, Mr. Victor Hoo, has significantly expanded his ownership position in the company through open-market purchases executed via his privately controlled investment vehicle.
  • The transactions, completed on November 21, 2025, were carried out in accordance with U.S. securities regulations, with full details to be furnished to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (“SEC”) via Form 6-K.
  • VCIG +4.31% premarket to $1.21.
  • Source: Press Release
